element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins with one or two settlers to found initial cities

element_introduction_order.element: City founding and basic terrain improvement
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Settlers build cities, then learn to mine and irrigate surrounding terrain
Player selects research priorities; technologies unlock new units and improvements
Barbarians emanate from huts and unclaimed land; frequency decreases as map is explored and claimed
Player can form alliances or declare war; computer opponents display civilization-specific traits
Combat emerges from technology advancement; enables conquest
Connected cities via roads; later railroads provide unlimited movement

element_introduction_order.element: Government forms and tax rate management
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player adjusts government type and taxation to balance growth and happiness
Endgame victory conditions; enables space colonization or mutual destruction

typical_relief_triggers: Successful city expansion and population growth
typical_tension_triggers: Competing civilizations researching key technologies first
Resource scarcity in newly founded cities
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-cycle tension with peaks during barbarian encounters and wars; underlying tension driven by technology race with AI opponents; relief through successful expansion and infrastructure development

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Unexplored terrain and huts contain barbarians; exploration gradually pacifies map regions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty: Player starts with settlers in safe territory; barbarian threat limited to exploration edges
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty: Competing civilizations expand simultaneously; military encounters and diplomatic tensions escalate; barbarian frequency decreases as map fills
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty: Endgame races for space/future technologies; late-game wars can be catastrophic; multiple rival civilizations at similar tech levels

notable_level_patterns.description: Player systematically explores unmapped regions to eliminate barbarian spawning locations; fog of war gradually lifts as scouts venture outward
Early technology choices create branching advancement paths; first-mover advantage on key techs (flight, gunpowder, nuclear) compounds over time
Player balances founding new cities (settler production) against improving existing cities (infrastructure, units); each choice affects long-term trajectory
Relationships with AI civilizations shift from alliance to rivalry based on relative military strength, technology parity, and territorial proximity
